root_cause: The React bundle could render before a reliable shared OTP client was available, and several pages used separate OTP implementations or direct API calls.
The same cache version had been reused across multiple patches, so mobile browsers could keep an older OTP client while loading a newer Contact component.
Signup, password reset, React Post Ad, and the fallback Post Ad page did not all use one client-side SMS state/service.
fix: Added one synchronous sms-verification-service.js loaded before the React bundle.
EHM_OTP is now only a compatibility alias to EHM_SMS.otp, so there is one shared state store.
Signup, account creation, password reset, contact-number OTP, resend, restore, and multi-number verification use the shared service.
The old otp-client.js is now a self-healing compatibility loader for old cached pages.
Added new cache versions and correct HTML/versioned-asset cache headers.
Preserved District/City data, login-before-posting, public ad browsing, and admin OTP switches.
